mode_rationale: Pipeline healthy (all 7 agents producing today, FUSE match 70/70, auth 200). No Mode 1/2 triggers. Previous report had no self-fixable items remaining. Deep cost analysis warranted.

previous_followup.digest_bug_line_144: STILL BROKEN — 7th consecutive escalation. Lines 147-148 in build-scripts/digest-reviews.js retain processedWinIds legacy fallback. The comment at lines 143-145 explicitly describes the bug but the code path remains. Impact reduced: pending digest is 0 entries, curmudgeon at 65/69. Writing PROP-003 this run.
previous_followup.prompt_bloat: MIXED — Tinker itself successfully converted to dispatcher (501→100 lines core). PROP-001/PROP-002 marked 'implemented' but analyst (436 lines) and decider (453 lines) unchanged. PROPs may have been prompt diet rather than dispatcher conversion.
previous_followup.future_dated_files: PERSISTS — suggested-patches-2026-04-09T10-01.json created on April 8. Cosmetic but indicates timezone offset in timestamp generation. Low priority.
previous_followup.PROP_001_002_effectiveness: INVESTIGATION — Both marked 'implemented' with targets analyst.md and decider.md, but line counts unchanged (436 and 453 respectively). Possible explanations: (1) diet was applied but content was added elsewhere, (2) status set prematurely. Need human verification.

agent_efficiency.recent_runs_checked: 3 reports: run1 (substantive — V51.1 analysis), run3 (substantive — updated analysis), run4 (explicit no-op: 'QUIET RUN. No new developments since run 3.')
agent_efficiency.substantive_output: Run 1-3 productive with dome V51.1 analysis. Run 4 was pure waste.
agent_efficiency.recommendation: Why 3 runs on a daily schedule? Either schedule was changed or extra runs were triggered manually. Run 4 was explicitly a no-op. A Haiku pre-flight gate (Pattern 2) would catch this: check if dome site has new commits since last social run; if not, skip.
Acceptable waste level for early detection role.
Already on cheapest model. Low overhead.
Dispatcher conversion complete (501→100 lines). Modules add ~428 lines but only one loads per run. Self-audit: this run is substantive (cost engineering findings + PROP-003).

expansion_tracker_discrepancy.finding: Expansion tracker shows 35 'complete' and 0 'integrated', but decider report from 2026-04-09T06-30 confirms EXP-036 and EXP-037 were integrated with commit 9c1c324.
expansion_tracker_discrepancy.impact: Decider may re-process already-integrated expansions, wasting tokens on dedup checks.
expansion_tracker_discrepancy.root_cause: Decider likely integrates content but doesn't update expansion-tracker.json status to 'integrated'. Or FUSE staleness masks the update.
expansion_tracker_discrepancy.recommendation: Verify in clean clone whether tracker has 'integrated' status for any items. If not, decider prompt needs explicit tracker update step after integration.

proposals_written.summary: Fix digest-reviews.js line 147-148 processedWinIds legacy fallback

cost_engineering.cumulative_estimated_savings: Tinker dispatcher saves ~400 lines of context per run. Analyst + decider dispatcher conversion (when implemented) would save ~700 additional lines across their combined runs.

digest_bug_analysis.description: Legacy processedWinIds fallback causes Cycle 2 reviews to be silently skipped if the base WIN ID was processed in Cycle 1. The code comment at lines 143-145 explicitly describes this bug, yet the fallback remains.
digest_bug_analysis.current_impact: Low (pending digest is 0, curmudgeon at 65/69). But any new Cycle 2+ reviews for already-processed WIN IDs will be masked.
digest_bug_analysis.fix: Remove processedWinIds entirely. Line 147-148 should be: const isProcessed = processedSet.has(file); — filename-only matching is sufficient and cycle-aware by design.

v6_restructure_audit: Clean. No stale section references found in recent agent outputs, patches, or tracker entries.
